domain_experience: senior data analyst at an ecommerce SaaS company
exposure to recurring merchant reporting pain across multiple accounts
assets: ability to translate reporting issues into operational fixes
network_access: peers in ecommerce and analytics roles
potential second-degree access to agencies and fractional operators
constraints: must avoid employer-confidential data or customer-specific IP
credibility_signals: clear domain expertise in merchant reporting pain
unfair_advantages: knows how reporting fails in real ecommerce workflows
can identify painful KPI trust issues faster than a generalist

binding_constraints: direct software sales are constrained by weak founder and budget-holder access
any offer must avoid employer-confidential methods, merchant data, and internal artifacts

opportunities.title: Productized ecommerce reporting teardown and fix sprint
opportunities.thesis: Sell a narrow audit-plus-implementation sprint that fixes one painful reporting bottleneck in 1-2 weeks.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has strong domain insight into recurring merchant reporting failures and can diagnose issues faster than a generic consultant.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If the offer stays broad or buyers do not feel enough urgency, it becomes generic analytics consulting.
validation_plan: Test the offer with 6 discovery calls in 4 days.
Seek 1 paid pilot in the $2000-$5000 range.
tool_specs: Simple intake form for stack and KPI pain points
Repeatable checklist for metric definition and data-source failures
Issue log and exception review template
kill_criteria: No paid pilot after 8 relevant conversations
Offer remains too custom to describe in one page

opportunities.title: Agency-facing ecommerce analytics QA service
opportunities.thesis: Sell recurring metric QA and dashboard cleanup support to agencies or fractional operators who already serve merchants.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This uses the user's existing peer network and domain fluency while reducing the need for direct founder selling.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Agencies may push for low-margin white-label work or may already have internal analytics coverage.
validation_plan: Ask 10 peers for referrals to agencies or fractional operators.
Secure 2 warm intros to merchant accounts.
kill_criteria: Fewer than 2 warm buyer intros after 10 outreach attempts
Partner economics are too low to sustain a product ladder

opportunities.title: Narrow micro-SaaS for metric QA and reporting trust
opportunities.thesis: Build a focused tool for broken metrics, inconsistent KPI definitions, and reporting anomalies after validating a repeated workflow.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has enough domain taste to choose a real wedge instead of building another generic dashboard.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Current distribution to budget owners is weak and the market is crowded with analytics tools.
validation_plan: Do not start until 3 paid pilots are complete.
Track whether at least 70% of issue detection repeats across accounts.
Confirm 3 buyers want ongoing monitoring, not just one-off cleanup.
tool_specs: Exception queue for reporting trust issues
kill_criteria: No repeated workflow after 4-6 paid engagements
Buyers prefer ad hoc services over recurring monitoring

opportunities.title: Reporting triage service to channel-led metric QA micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a narrow reporting trust service sold through agencies and operators, then productize the repeated QA workflow into software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This fits the user's strongest edge, problem knowledge, while compensating for weak direct founder distribution through partner channels.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If the work remains bespoke or no partner channel converts into repeated demand, the product wedge never becomes clean.
validation_plan: Build a one-page offer and contact 30 relevant people in 7 days.
Book at least 8 conversations and pitch 3 pilots.
Proceed only with 1 paid pilot or a highly specific pending pilot plus 2 warm buyer intros.
Track repeated issue types and requests for ongoing monitoring.
tool_specs: Exception tracker and stakeholder-facing issue summaries
Future product wedge: definition registry plus discrepancy alerts
kill_criteria: Fewer than 8 relevant conversations in 7 days
Fewer than 2 partner or operator intros to real buyers
No paid pilot by the end of the first validation cycle
Less than 70% workflow overlap after 4-6 engagements

rejected_paths.title: Software-first ecommerce analytics micro-SaaS
rejected_paths.reason: Too weak on current distribution and too crowded to justify building before paid validation.

rejected_paths.title: Broad analytics consulting
rejected_paths.reason: Too custom and weakly differentiated to create a durable asset later.

next_actions: Draft a one-page Ecommerce Reporting Trust Sprint offer.
Build a list of 30 peers, agencies, and fractional operators for outreach.
Pitch 3 pilots and seek 1 paid engagement.
Track issue overlap and requests for ongoing monitoring before building software.
